content_en:
is more precious to me than all you have brought. I could have killed you, but I would hate for this to be held as a precedent. Go pay his family bloodmoney and do not let me see your face again.’ " (Reported by Al-Baihaqi.)

It is reported that a man left his wife behind in San* aa’, leaving to her care her stepson, called Aseel. Now that she was alone, the woman had a_ lover take her husband's place. The woman one day told her lover, "This boy may disclose our secret, so kill him." But the man refused to. Thereupon, the woman would not answer her lover's calls, forcing him to yield to her demand. The young boy was hence murdered by the lover, another man, and the woman along with her maid; they all butchered him into pieces and put the parts in a leather bag and dumped it into a dry well nearby the village. The lover was afterwards arrested; he confessed to the crime and so did the rest of the murderers, Yala - then the governor — wrote Umar informing him of their crime. ~ Umar ordered them to be all put to death and said: "I swear that if all of the residents of San*aa’ had taken part in killing the boy, I would have killed them all." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

“Umar's Unique Loyalty and Gratitude:

“Umar set an example in loyalty and gratitude. Aslam narrates: "Once I went with “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab to the market. A young woman followed him and said: 'O Commander of the Faithful! My husband died, leaving me little children. By Allah, they have not even a sheep's trotter to eat; they have no farms or animals. | am afraid that they may starve to death, and I am the daughter of Khufaf [bn Imaa' Al-Ghafari, and my father witnessed the Pledge of allegiance of AlHudaybiyah with the Prophet.’ ~Umar stood still and said, 'I welcome my near relatives.' Then he went towards a strong camel which was tied in the house, and carried on it two sacks

he had loaded with grains and put between them money and clothes, then gave her its rope to hold and said: ‘Lead it, and this provision will last long enough till Allah grants you a good supply.'. A man said, ‘O Commander of the Faithful! You have given her too much." '* Umar said disapprovingly: 'May your mother be bereaved of you! By Allah, I saw her father and brother besiege a fort for a long time and conquer it, and then we started discussing what our shares would be from the war booty." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

Similarly, ‘Abdullaah Ibn *Abbaas narrates: " “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab departed for Syria and when he reached Sargh, the commanders of Muslim army, Abu *Ubaydah Ibn AlJarraaah and his companions met him and told him that an epidemic had broken out in the Levant. ~ Umar said, ‘Call the Muhajireen.' So ‘Umar consulted them and informed them that an epidemic had broken out in the Levant. They differed in their opinions; some of them pointed: 'We have come for a certain purpose and we do not think that it is proper to give it up.' Others however said (to ~ Umar): 'There have come along with you other people as well as the companions of Allah's Messenger, so we think we should not take them to this epidemic." So ‘Umar told them, 'Leave me now.' He then said, ‘Call the Ansaar for me." | called them, he consulted them and like the Muhajireen, they were divided on the issue. He then said to them, 'Leave me now,' and added, ‘Call for me the old men of Quraysh who emigrated in the year of the Conquest of Makkah.' I called them and they gave a unanimous opinion saying: ‘Our advice is that you should return with the people and do not take them to the place of the epidemic.’ So ~ Umar made an announcement: ‘I will ride back to Madinah in the morning, so you should do the same.’ Abu * Ubaydah Ibn AlJarraaah asked (Umar): ‘Are you running away from what Allah had predestined?’ ~Umar simply answered: 'Would that someone else had said such a thing, O Abu * Ubaydah! Yes, we

are running from what Allah had predestined to what Allah has predestined. Do you not agree that if you had camels that went down a_ valley having two places, one green and the other dry, you would graze them on the green one only if Allah had ordained that, and you would graze them on the dry one only if Allah had ordained that?' At that point, * Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn *Awf, who had been absent because of some job, showed up and said: 1 have some knowledge about this. I have heard Allah's Messenger say, ‘Jf you hear about it (an outbreak of plague) in a land, do not go to it; but if a plague breaks out in a country where you are staying, do not run away from it.' “Umar thanked Allah and returned to Madinah." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

“Umar was also the very embodiment of piety and genuine fear of Allah, Exalted be He. No words could possibly do justice to his feelings of piety and devoutness. While ~Uthmaan Ibn ~ Affaan was conducting business at Al-* Aaliyah on a scorching hot day, he spotted a man steering two sturdy male camels; the grounsd was as if it was paved with fire. “Uthmaan wondered: “The man could have stayed in the city till the weather cooled down, then set out on his trip!” ~Uthmaan ‘s servant looked closely and told his master, "You see who this man is?" ~Uthmaan took a good look and said: "I can see a man who has pulled his garment over his head, steering two sturdy male camels." The servant looked even closer and said, "Look!" *~Uthmaan looked again only to see “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab! He said in amazement, "This is the Commander of the Faithful!" ~Uthmaan got up, opened the door and put out his head. He was lashed by the Samoom wind and so immediately withdrew his head. When ~Umar passed before him, ~Uthmaan asked him, “Whatever brings you out now?" ~Umar answered: “Two sturdy males of the charity

camels were left behind, and the other camels have already been marched. I wanted to catch up with them at the pasture preserve lest they should be lost and Allah will surely ask me about them." *Uthmaan said: "O Commander of the Faithful, have some water and shelter and we will take care of this." “Umar returned, "Go back to your shelter, ~ Uthmaan." ~ Uthmaan then said: "Whoever wishes to set eyes on a powerful honest man, let him look at “Umar; he was then gripped with sorrow."

(Reported by Ibn Al-Atheer.)

*Abdullaah Ibn ~Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both, narrates: "I bought camels and sent them to the pasture preserve. When they fattened, I brought them back. One day “Umar walked into the market and saw some fat camels. He asked, 'Who do they belong to?’ He was told, 'They belong to *Abdullaah Ibn ~Umar.' ~Umar then kept repeating: 'O “Abdullaah! Well done! Well done! Son of the Commander of the Faithful!’ I hurried to him and asked, ‘What is wrong, Commander of the Faithful?’ He asked me, ‘What about those camels?' I answered: 'They are some skinny camels I bought and sent to the pastures seeking what all Muslims do.’ ~ Umar returned: ‘Let the camels of the son of the Commander of the Faithful graze! Water the camels of the son of the Commander of the Faithful! Take your capital, ~ Abdullaah Ibn ~ Umar and direct the profit to Muslims’ treasury.'" (Quoted from Akhbaar ~Umar.)

Ibn ~Abbaas, may Allah be pleased with them both, reports: "* Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab asked me to go to him, which I did. There was spread in front of him a leather carpet all covered with gold. He said, ‘Go ahead and distribute this money among your people. Allah has kept such treasures from His Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and from Abu Bakr and gave it to me. Only He knows best whether it is a blessing or a curse?’ I fell to distributing it and separating

shares. Yet, I heard a man weep. It turned out to be * Umar weeping and saying: 'l swear by Him in Whose Hands my soul is, Allah has not kept this from His Prophet or from Abu Bakr by way of affliction, nor did He give it to “Umar by way of a blessing.'" (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.)

It is narrated that the “Umar used to place his hand close to fire and say: "Are you able to tolerate this, Ibn AlKhattaab?" (Quoted from Akhbaar *Umar.)

“Ali Ibn Abu Taalib, may Allah be pleased with him, states: "I saw ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, riding a small camel. I asked, ‘Where to, Commander of the Faithful?’ He answered, ‘One of the charity camels has fled.' I rejoined, "You have humiliated all your successors.’ He said, 'Do not blame me, Abu Al-Hasan. If a young she-goat were to be killed on the Euphrates coast, “Umar would be punished for it on Judgment Day.'"

Qatadah reports: Mu’ aikib was in charge of the Treasury during ~Umar's caliphate. One day he prepared to distribute all the money in the Treasury only to find a single Dirham in it, so he sent it to Ibn ~Umar. Mu aikib says, "I then went home. Shortly afterwards, ~“Umar's messenger arrived asking me to meet him. I walked in on him only to find the Dirham in the palm of his hand. *Umar asked, 'How can you do that, Mu aikib? Do you have any case against me? Is it your money or mine?’ I returned, ‘What is wrong?’ He answered: ‘Do you want the entire nation of Muhammad to hold me accountable for this Dirham?' " (Reported by Ibn Al-Jawzi.)

“Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab was an example to be followed in humbleness. Similarly, he led a harsh life of austerity and

lived on coarse food. The one single goal he aspired to was Paradise. “Umar used to patch his clothes with the skin of camels. Notwithstanding his lofty, awe-striking status, ~ Umar never hesitated to carry a water bottle on his shoulders. He would ride a donkey with no saddle and a riding camel bridled with fiber. He rarely laughed or joked with anyone. He wore a ting on which was engraved, "Death Teaches the Best Lesson, *Umar."

Qatadah reports: "When * Umar arrived in Syria, he was brought food, the like of which he had never tasted before. He therefore said: ‘This is for us? What about poor Muslims who live on barley bread, which never satisfies their hunger?’ Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, may Allah be pleased with him, answered, ‘They will be rewarded with Paradise.’ *Umar's eyes thereupon were filled with tears, and he said: 'If this is our share, while they end up winning Paradise, they are way ahead of us." (Reported by Ibn Al-Jawzi.)
content_id:
lebih berharga bagiku daripada semua yang kau bawa. Aku bisa membunuhmu, namun aku tidak ingin hal ini dijadikan preseden. Pergi bayar diyat kepada keluarganya dan jangan biarkan aku melihat wajahmu lagi.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihaqi.)

Diriwayatkan bahwa seorang pria meninggalkan istrinya di Shan'aa', mempercayakan kepadanya perawatan anak tirinya yang bernama Aseel. Karena sendirian, wanita itu mengambil seorang kekasih sebagai pengganti suaminya. Suatu hari wanita itu berkata kepada kekasihnya, "Anak ini mungkin akan membocorkan rahasia kita, maka bunuhlah dia." Namun pria itu menolak. Kemudian wanita itu tidak mau menemui kekasihnya, memaksanya untuk menuruti permintaannya. Anak muda itu pun dibunuh oleh sang kekasih, seorang pria lain, dan wanita itu bersama pembantunya; mereka semua mencincangnya menjadi potongan-potongan dan memasukkan bagian-bagian tubuhnya ke dalam kantong kulit lalu membuangnya ke dalam sumur kering di dekat desa. Sang kekasih kemudian ditangkap; ia mengakui kejahatan tersebut dan begitu pula para pembunuh lainnya. Ya'la — yang saat itu menjadi gubernur — menulis surat kepada Umar memberitahukan tentang kejahatan mereka. Umar memerintahkan agar mereka semua dihukum mati dan berkata: "Aku bersumpah bahwa seandainya seluruh penduduk Shan'aa' ikut serta dalam membunuh anak itu, niscaya aku akan membunuh mereka semua." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

"Kesetiaan dan Rasa Syukur Umar yang Luar Biasa:

"Umar menjadi teladan dalam kesetiaan dan rasa syukur. Aslam bercerita: "Suatu ketika aku pergi bersama Umar Ibn Al-Khattab ke pasar. Seorang wanita muda mengikutinya dan berkata: 'Wahai Amirul Mukminin! Suamiku telah meninggal, meninggalkan anak-anak kecil bersamaku. Demi Allah, mereka bahkan tidak memiliki kaki domba untuk dimakan; mereka tidak memiliki ladang maupun hewan ternak. Aku khawatir mereka akan mati kelaparan, dan aku adalah putri Khufaf Ibn Imaa' Al-Ghafari, dan ayahku menyaksikan Baiat Al-Hudaybiyah bersama Nabi.' Umar berdiri diam dan berkata, 'Selamat datang, saudaraku yang dekat.' Kemudian ia menuju seekor unta kuat yang diikat di rumah, dan memuat dua karung

yang telah diisinya dengan biji-bijian dan menaruh uang serta pakaian di antaranya, kemudian memberikan tali untanya kepadanya seraya berkata: 'Tuntunlah unta ini, dan bekal ini akan cukup hingga Allah memberikan rezeki yang baik kepadamu.' Seorang pria berkata, 'Wahai Amirul Mukminin! Engkau telah memberinya terlalu banyak.' Umar berkata dengan tidak setuju: 'Semoga ibumu meratapi kepergianmu! Demi Allah, aku melihat ayah dan saudaranya mengepung sebuah benteng dalam waktu lama dan menaklukkannya, lalu kami mulai membicarakan bagian kami dari harta rampasan perang.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Demikian pula, Abdullah Ibn Abbas meriwayatkan: "Umar Ibn Al-Khattab berangkat menuju Syam dan ketika sampai di Sargh, para panglima pasukan Muslim, Abu Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarrah dan para sahabatnya menemuinya dan memberitahunya bahwa wabah telah melanda Syam. Umar berkata, 'Panggillah kaum Muhajirin.' Maka Umar bermusyawarah dengan mereka dan memberitahukan bahwa wabah telah melanda Syam. Mereka berselisih pendapat; sebagian dari mereka menunjukkan: 'Kami telah datang untuk suatu tujuan dan kami tidak berpendapat bahwa kami harus meninggalkannya.' Sementara yang lain berkata (kepada Umar): 'Bersamamu ada orang-orang lain serta para sahabat Rasulullah, maka kami berpendapat sebaiknya kita tidak membawa mereka ke tempat wabah ini.' Maka Umar berkata kepada mereka, 'Tinggalkan aku sekarang.' Kemudian ia berkata, 'Panggilkan kaum Anshar untukku.' Aku memanggil mereka, ia bermusyawarah dengan mereka dan seperti kaum Muhajirin, mereka pun terbagi pendapatnya. Kemudian ia berkata kepada mereka, 'Tinggalkan aku sekarang,' dan menambahkan, 'Panggilkan untukku para tetua Quraisy yang berhijrah pada tahun Penaklukan Makkah.' Aku memanggil mereka dan mereka memberikan pendapat yang bulat seraya berkata: 'Saran kami adalah hendaknya engkau kembali bersama orang-orang dan jangan membawa mereka ke tempat wabah.' Maka Umar mengumumkan: 'Aku akan kembali ke Madinah pada pagi hari, maka lakukanlah hal yang sama.' Abu Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarrah bertanya (kepada Umar): 'Apakah engkau lari dari apa yang telah Allah takdirkan?' Umar menjawab dengan sederhana: 'Seandainya orang lain yang mengatakannya, wahai Abu Ubaidah! Ya, kita

berlari dari apa yang telah Allah takdirkan menuju apa yang Allah takdirkan. Tidakkah engkau setuju bahwa seandainya engkau memiliki unta yang turun ke lembah yang memiliki dua tempat, yang satu hijau dan yang lain kering, engkau akan menggembalakan mereka di tempat yang hijau hanya jika Allah telah menentukannya, dan engkau akan menggembalakan mereka di tempat yang kering hanya jika Allah telah menentukannya?' Saat itu, Abdurrahman Ibn Awf, yang tidak hadir karena suatu urusan, muncul dan berkata: 'Aku memiliki pengetahuan tentang hal ini. Aku telah mendengar Rasulullah bersabda, "Jika kamu mendengar tentangnya (wabah penyakit) di suatu negeri, janganlah pergi ke sana; tetapi jika wabah melanda negeri tempat kamu berada, janganlah lari darinya."' Umar memuji Allah dan kembali ke Madinah." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Umar juga merupakan perwujudan nyata dari ketakwaan dan rasa takut yang tulus kepada Allah 'Azza wa Jalla. Tidak ada kata-kata yang mampu menggambarkan dengan tepat perasaan taqwa dan ibadahnya. Ketika Utsman Ibn Affan sedang melakukan perniagaan di Al-'Aliyah pada hari yang sangat terik, ia melihat seorang pria menggiring dua ekor unta jantan yang kuat; tanah seolah-olah seperti dilapisi api. Utsman bertanya-tanya: "Orang itu bisa saja tetap tinggal di kota hingga cuaca mendingin, kemudian baru berangkat dalam perjalanannya!" Pelayan Utsman menatap lebih seksama dan berkata kepada tuannya, "Tahukah tuan siapa pria ini?" Utsman memandang dengan seksama dan berkata: "Aku melihat seorang pria yang menarik pakaiannya di atas kepalanya, menggiring dua ekor unta jantan yang kuat." Pelayan itu memandang lebih dekat lagi dan berkata, "Lihatlah!" Utsman melihat lagi dan mengenali Umar Ibn Al-Khattab! Ia berkata dengan takjub, "Ini Amirul Mukminin!" Utsman bangkit, membuka pintu dan menjulurkan kepalanya keluar. Ia disambar oleh angin Samoom dan segera menarik kembali kepalanya. Ketika Umar lewat di hadapannya, Utsman bertanya kepadanya, "Apa yang membawamu keluar sekarang?" Umar menjawab: "Dua ekor unta jantan dari unta-unta zakat

tertinggal, sementara unta-unta lainnya sudah diarak. Aku ingin mengejar mereka di padang penggembalaan agar tidak hilang dan Allah pasti akan menanyaiku tentang mereka." Utsman berkata: "Wahai Amirul Mukminin, minumlah air dan berlindunglah di sini, kami akan mengurus hal ini." Umar menjawab, "Kembalilah ke tempat berlindungmu, Utsman." Utsman kemudian berkata: "Barangsiapa yang ingin melihat seorang pria yang kuat dan jujur, maka lihatlah Umar; ia kemudian diliputi rasa kesedihan."

(Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Al-Atsir.)

Abdullah Ibn Umar, semoga Allah meridhai keduanya, meriwayatkan: "Aku membeli unta-unta dan mengirimkan mereka ke padang penggembalaan. Ketika mereka telah gemuk, aku membawa mereka kembali. Suatu hari Umar berjalan ke pasar dan melihat beberapa ekor unta yang gemuk. Ia bertanya, 'Milik siapa ini?' Dikatakan kepadanya, 'Milik Abdullah Ibn Umar.' Umar kemudian terus mengulang-ulang: 'Wahai Abdullah! Bagus sekali! Bagus sekali! Putra Amirul Mukminin!' Aku bergegas menemuinya dan bertanya, 'Ada apa, Amirul Mukminin?' Ia bertanya kepadaku, 'Bagaimana dengan unta-unta itu?' Aku menjawab: 'Mereka adalah beberapa ekor unta kurus yang kubeli dan kukirim ke padang penggembalaan sebagaimana yang dilakukan semua kaum Muslimin.' Umar menjawab: 'Biarkanlah unta-unta putra Amirul Mukminin merumput! Berilah minum unta-unta putra Amirul Mukminin! Ambillah modalmu, Abdullah Ibn Umar, dan serahkanlah keuntungannya ke baitul mal kaum Muslimin.'" (Dikutip dari Akhbar Umar.)

Ibn Abbas, semoga Allah meridhai keduanya, melaporkan: "Umar Ibn Al-Khattab memintaku untuk menemuinya, dan aku pun pergi. Di hadapannya terbentang sebuah permadani kulit yang dipenuhi emas. Ia berkata, 'Pergilah dan bagikan harta ini kepada kaummu. Allah telah menyimpan kekayaan seperti ini dari Nabi-Nya, semoga shalawat dan salam tercurah atasnya, dan dari Abu Bakr, lalu memberikannya kepadaku. Hanya Dia yang paling mengetahui apakah ini merupakan berkah atau musibah?' Aku pun mulai membagi-bagikannya dan memisahkan

bagian-bagian. Namun, aku mendengar seorang pria menangis. Ternyata itu adalah Umar yang menangis sambil berkata: 'Aku bersumpah demi Dzat yang jiwa ini berada di tangan-Nya, Allah tidak menyimpan ini dari Nabi-Nya atau dari Abu Bakr sebagai bentuk penderitaan, dan Dia tidak memberikannya kepada Umar sebagai bentuk berkah.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Sa'd.)

Diriwayatkan bahwa Umar biasa mendekatkan tangannya ke api dan berkata: "Apakah engkau mampu menanggung ini, Ibn Al-Khattab?" (Dikutip dari Akhbar Umar.)

Ali Ibn Abu Thalib, semoga Allah meridhainya, menyatakan: "Aku melihat Umar, semoga Allah meridhainya, menunggangi seekor unta kecil. Aku bertanya, 'Hendak ke mana, Amirul Mukminin?' Ia menjawab, 'Salah satu unta zakat telah melarikan diri.' Aku menyambung, 'Engkau telah membuat malu semua penerusmu.' Ia berkata, 'Jangan mencelaku, Abu Al-Hasan. Seandainya seekor anak kambing betina terbunuh di tepi sungai Eufrat, Umar pasti akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas hal itu pada Hari Kiamat.'"

Qatadah meriwayatkan: Mu'aikib bertugas mengawasi Baitul Mal pada masa kekhalifahan Umar. Suatu hari ia bersiap untuk membagikan seluruh uang di Baitul Mal, namun hanya mendapati satu Dirham di dalamnya, maka ia mengirimkannya kepada Ibnu Umar. Mu'aikib berkata, "Aku lalu pulang ke rumah. Tidak lama kemudian, utusan Umar datang memintaku untuk menemuinya. Aku masuk dan mendapati Dirham itu berada di telapak tangannya. Umar bertanya, 'Bagaimana engkau bisa melakukan itu, Mu'aikib? Apakah engkau punya urusan denganku? Apakah ini uangmu atau uangku?' Aku menjawab, 'Ada apa gerangan?' Beliau menjawab: 'Apakah engkau ingin seluruh umat Muhammad meminta pertanggungjawabanku atas satu Dirham ini?' " (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Al-Jawzi.)

Umar Ibn Al-Khattab adalah teladan yang patut diikuti dalam hal kerendahan hati. Demikian pula, beliau menjalani kehidupan yang keras penuh kesederhanaan dan

hidup dengan makanan yang sederhana. Satu-satunya tujuan yang beliau dambakan hanyalah Surga. Umar biasa menambal pakaiannya dengan kulit unta. Meskipun memiliki kedudukan yang tinggi dan berwibawa, Umar tidak pernah segan memikul kantong air di bahunya. Beliau menunggang keledai tanpa pelana dan unta tunggangan yang ditali dengan serat. Beliau jarang tertawa atau bercanda dengan siapapun. Beliau memakai cincin yang terukir padanya, "Kematian Mengajarkan Pelajaran Terbaik, Umar."

Qatadah meriwayatkan: "Ketika Umar tiba di Syam, dihidangkan kepadanya makanan yang belum pernah ia rasakan sebelumnya. Maka beliau berkata: 'Ini untuk kita? Bagaimana dengan kaum Muslimin yang miskin yang hidup dari roti gandum kasar yang tidak pernah mengenyangkan mereka?' Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, radhiyallahu 'anhu, menjawab, 'Mereka akan dibalas dengan Surga.' Mata Umar pun serta-merta berlinang air mata, dan beliau berkata: 'Jika ini adalah bagian kita, sementara mereka berakhir memenangkan Surga, maka mereka jauh lebih unggul dari kita.' " (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Al-Jawzi.)
content_ar: is more precious to me than all you have brought. I could have killed you, but I would الكراهية for this to be held as a precedent. Go pay his الأسرة bloodmoney and do not let me see your face again.’ " (Reported by Al-Baihaqi.) وردت الرواية أنه a man left his الزوجة behind in San* aa’, leaving to her care her stepson, called Aseel. Now that she was alone, the woman had a_ lover take her الزوج's place. The woman يوماً ما told her lover, "This boy may disclose our secret, لذا kill him." But the man refused to. Thereupon, the woman would not answer her lover's calls, forcing him to yield to her demand. The young boy was ومن ثَم murdered by the lover, another man, and the woman along with her maid; they all butchered him into pieces and put the parts in a leather bag and dumped it into a dry well nearby the village. The lover was afterwards arrested; he confessed to the crime and لذا did the rest of the murderers, Yala - then the governor — wrote عمر informing him of their crime. ~ عمر ordered them to be all put to الموت and said: "I swear that إذا all of the residents of San*aa’ had taken part in killing the boy, I would have killed them all." (Reported by البخاري.) “عمر's Unique Loyalty and الشكر: “عمر set an example in loyalty and الشكر. Aslam narrates: "حين I went with “عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab to the market. A young woman followed him and said: 'O Commander of the Faithful! My الزوج died, leaving me little الأولاد. By الله, they have not even a sheep's trotter to eat; they have no farms or animals. | am afraid that they may starve to الموت, and I am the daughter of Khufaf [bn Imaa' Al-Ghafari, and my الأب witnessed the Pledge of allegiance of AlHudaybiyah with النبي ﷺ.’ ~عمر stood still and said, 'I welcome my near relatives.' Then he went towards a strong camel which was tied in the house, and carried on it two sacks he had loaded with grains and put between them المال and clothes, then gave her its rope to hold and said: ‘Lead it, and this provision will last long enough till الله grants you a الخير supply.'. A man said, ‘O Commander of the Faithful! You have given her أيضاً much." '* عمر said disapprovingly: 'May your الأم be bereaved of you! By الله, I saw her الأب and الأخ besiege a fort for a long time and conquer it, and then we started discussing what our shares would be from the war booty." (Reported by البخاري.) Similarly, ‘Abdullaah Ibn *Abbaas narrates: " “عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab departed for Syria and عندما he reached Sargh, the commanders of مسلم army, Abu *Ubaydah Ibn AlJarraaah and his الصحابة met him and told him that an epidemic had broken out in the Levant. ~ عمر said, ‘Call the Muhajireen.' لذا ‘عمر consulted them and informed them that an epidemic had broken out in the Levant. They differed in their opinions; some of them pointed: 'We have come for a certain purpose and we do not think that it is proper to give it up.' Others غير أن said (to ~ عمر): 'There
